text.title: Verify-review-fix iteration with hard cap
text.summary: After execution merges, the orchestrator runs a bounded loop: build/test verifier → independent reviewer → fix-applier on blockers, repeating until the reviewer returns clean or the iteration cap forces escalation.

list.item: VERIFY: build, type-check, lint, unit tests, smoke checks, and (for knowledge methodologies) `python3 scripts/validate-methodology-xml.py <path>`.
VERIFY (e2e): Playwright when the feature's `test-plan.md` calls for it (`faion-net-e2e/`, storybook visual regression).
REVIEW: an independent reviewer subagent reads the diff against `spec.md` / `design.md` / `test-plan.md` and returns a verdict.
FIX: if the verdict has blockers, the fix-applier addresses each one inside the same worktree, then the loop restarts at VERIFY.
Knowledge methodology (`skills/faion/knowledge/...`): `python3 scripts/validate-methodology-xml.py <path>` PASS, plus pre-commit `CHANGELOG.md` check.
Backend (`faion-net-be/`): `ruff check . --fix && ruff format .`, `pytest`, Django system check.
Frontend (`faion-net-fe/`, `mediamanager_fe/`, gatsby pipelines): `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`.
E2E (`faion-net-e2e/`): `npm run test` (Playwright suite scoped to the feature's test plan).
Storybook (`faion-network-storybook/`): `npm run build-storybook` and visual regression where configured.
DAG / pipeline (`dag/` style projects): `ruff check . --fix`, `pytest`, smoke run on a fixture dataset.
FAIL-WITH-NITS — only nice-to-have items; orchestrator may accept and proceed (records the nits as TODOs in the feature's `done/` move).
FAIL — at least one blocker; loop continues.

rule.statement: The reviewer subagent MUST be a separate `Agent` spawn from the executor. It receives only the spec/design/test-plan and the diff, never the executor's reasoning.
rule.rationale: An author rarely catches their own structural mistakes. Independence is what makes the review a meaningful check, not a rubber stamp.

rule.statement: The verify-review-fix loop MUST terminate at three iterations. After three, the orchestrator stops and asks the user how to proceed via `AskUserQuestion`.
rule.rationale: An infinite loop hides a real disagreement between the spec and the implementation. After three rounds, escalation to a human is cheaper than another machine round-trip.

list.item: Addresses blocker findings only. Nice-to-haves are deferred unless the orchestrator explicitly asks.
One commit per logical group (security findings together, code-quality findings together).
Must not refactor unrelated code while fixing — scope creep invalidates the review.
If a haiku-driven attempt fails the same review twice, the orchestrator MUST retry with `model: sonnet` before escalating.
